TREES THROUGH A HOME IN SANFORD. NUMEROUS TREES DOWN ACROSS THE CITY.
TREES DOWN AND MINOR DAMAGE REPORTED TO THE ROOF OF A HOUSE JUST TO THE NORTH OF ELON AT 1034 SHALLOWFORD CHURCH RD.

SEVRAL STRUCTURES WERE DAMAGED AND NUMEROUS TREES REPORTED DOWN NEAR WILLIE PACE RD NEAR UNION RIDGE... AND A BARN WAS DESTROYED NEAR VISON RD NEAR THE ALAMANCE/CASWELL COUNTY LINE. AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN NORTHERN ALAMANCE COUNTY A HALF MILE SOUTH OF WILLIE PACE RD NEAR UNION RIDGE AND TRACKED TO THE NORTHEAST FOR APPROXIMATELY TWO MILES TO THE ALAMANCE/CASWELL COUNTY LINE. THE TORNADO HAD A DAMAGE WIDTH OF 100 YARDS WIDE AND WAS RESPONSABLE FOR THE AFOREMENTIONED DAMAGE.

A WEAK TORNADO PRODUCED TREE DAMAGE AND TOOK SHINGLES OFF ONE HOME NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF THIRTEEN BRIDGES ROAD AND DOUGLAS HILL FARM ROAD. AFTER PRODUCING THE TREE AND ROOF DAMAGE... THE TORNADO TRACKED INTO A SWAMPY AREA WHERE IT THEN LIFTED BACK UP. THIS TORNADO WAS RATED AS AN EF-0. PATH LENGTH WAS ABOUT ONE MILE.

AT 312 PM EDT...A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN WEST OF PRINCETON ROAD...JUST NORTH OF OLD SMITHFIELD ROAD. A GARAGE DOOR WAS BLOWN IN ON A BRICK HOUSE. THE ROOF OF ONE MOBILE HOME WAS BLOWN OFF...AND ANOTHER MOBILE HOME ROLLED 20 YARDS ACROSS A FIELD...AND STRUCK A THIRD MOBILE HOME. FINALLY...A FOURTH MOBILE HOME WAS PARTIALLY BLOWN FROM ITS FOUNDATION. THERE WERE NO INJURIES.

FLASH FLOODING OCCURRED AS MARSH CREEK FLOODED BRENTWOOD PARK. THE SOFTBALL FIELD AND TRAIL ALONG THE CREEK IN BRENTWOOD PARK WERE FLOODED BY 4-5 FEET OF RAPIDLY FLOWING WATER. FLOODING BEGAN AROUND 730 PM... REACHING AT PEAK AT 900 PM.
FLASH FLOODING OCCURRED AT CAPITAL BLVD/BRENTWOOD ROAD... AS MARSH CREEK FLOODED THE SOUTH BOUND LANES OF CAPITAL BLVD WITH NEARLY 3 FEET OF WATER AT THE INTERSECTION WITH BRENTWOOD ROAD.
SEVERAL TREES WERE BLOW DOWN ALONG BULLOCKSVILLE ROAD WEST OF THE TOWN OF DREWRY. ONE TREE CAUSED MINOR DAMAGE TO A HOME. SEVERAL TREES WERE ALSO BLOWN IN A NEARBY PARK ON KERR LAKE.

CABIN CREEK JUST NORTH OF DURHAM IS WELL OUT OF ITS BANKS. NORMALLY THE CREEK IS TWO TO THREE FEET WIDE. A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED THE CREEK NOW TO BE AS WIDE AS 150 FEET. THIS HAS RESULTED IN CABIN CREEK FLOODING US HWY 501 BETWEEN SMITH DRIVE AND NORTHERN HIGH SCHOOL.
A PONDS DAM HAS FAILED NEAR TRACEY TRAIL ROAD... JUST NORTH OF DURHAM OFF US HWY 501. THE HAS CAUSED EXTENSIVE FLOOLING IN THE EDEN LAKE SUBDIVISION.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED WEST C STREET IN BUTNER IS FLOODED.
A LARGE BARN WAS DESTROYED NEAR THE WAYNE/WILSON COUNTY LINE... IN WAYNE COUNTY ALONG AYCOCK CHURCH ROAD.
SEVERAL TREES REPORTED AND DOWN AND A BASKETBALL GOAL BLOWN OVER ALONG SOUTH BEAVER DAM ROAD... JUST NORTH OF THE WAYNE/WILSON COUNTY LINE.
SUNSET LAKE ROAD FLOODED... NCDOT ON THE SCENE.
DENNY STORE RD AND POLK HUFF RD WERE CLOSED DUE TO HIGH WATER.
ONE TREE REPORTED DOWN AND ACROSS UPCHURCH ROAD... NEAR FOUR TENTH ROAD.
QUARTER SIZE HAIL REPORTED IN CARY.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED SEVERAL TREES DOWN ACROSS MORRISVILLE CARPENTER ROAD... NEAR DAVIS DRIVE.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED MORRISVILLE CARPENTER ROAD TO BE FLOODED BETWEEN DAVIS DRIVE AND TOWN HALL DRIVE. THE FIRE DEPARTMENT HAS CLOSED THIS PORTIONS OF MORRISVILLE CARPENTER ROAD.
FLOODING AT SOUTH SAUNDERS AND MARTIN LUTHER KING BLVD AND ON AVENT FERRY ROAD NEAR NCSU.
COUNTY OFFICIALS REPORTED NUMEROUS ROAD FLOOD ACROSS THE CITY OF GOLDSBORO.
ONE TREE REPORTED DOWN ACROSS ROCK RIDGE SCHOOL ROAD... NEAR AMBER ROAD.
DRIVER RESCUED FROM VEHICLE AFTER DRIVING INTO FLOODED UNDERPASS AT HILLSBOROUGH/CHAPEL DRIVE IN WEST RALEIGH.
DRIVER RESCUED FROM VEHICLE AFTER DRIVING INTO FLOODED UNDERPASS AT HILLSBOROUGH/CHAPEL DRIVE IN WEST RALEIGH. CORRECTED EVENT TIME, RFD CALLED AT 148 AM.
NORTH KING STREET AND GROVE STREET IN DOWNTOWN FAYETTEVILLE IS IMPASSABLE DUE TO HIGH WATER.
SULLIVAN DR BETWEEN DAN ALLEN AND VARSITY DR ON THE NC STATE CAMPUS HAS BEEN CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING
AVENT FERRY RD NEAR TRAILWOOD DR IS BEING CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING
TREE DOWN ON APARTMENT BUILDING AT NAVAHO DRIVE. SEVERAL UNITS EVACUATED.
LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORT FLOODING IN THE RAEFORD GREEN APARTMENTS. WATER IS UP TO 3 FEET DEEP IN SEVERAL BUILDINGS.
A DOT CREW WAS DISPATCHED DUE TO HIGH STANDING WATER ON SNOWHILL AND SNOWVALLEY ROADS.
A DOT CREW WAS DISPATCHED TO MINERAL SPRINGS AND FLETCHERS CHAPEL ROAD DUE TO HIGH STANDING WATER ON ROAD.
911 CENTER REPORTS ROAD CLOSURES ALONG MIDLAND ROAD AND CENTRAL DRIVE. THE EXIT RAMP ON SOUTH BOUND US HWY 1 ALSO HAD TO BE CLOSED.
HOBSON ROAD IS IMPASSABLE... NEAR US HWY 401.
THE INTERSECTION OF OF SPRING LANE AND WILKINS DRIVE IS IMPASSABLE.
FLOODING OF SPORTS FIELDS AT DURHAM ACADEMY.
SEVERAL ROADS FLOODED AND CLOSED IN GOLDSBORO.
SEVERAL ROADS FLOODED AND CLOSED THROUGHOUT THE COUNTY.
SEVERAL ROADS CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING IN MOORE COUNTY INCLUDING SEVERAL RAMPS TO US 1.
FLOODING IN SEVERAL AREAS ALONG NC-54.
AT THE INTERSECTION OF ERWIN AND MT.MORIAH THERE WAS A REPORT OF STANDING WATER...ROAD IMPASSABLE.
EVACUATIONS BEGINNING DUE TO FLASH FLOODING AT THE CAMELOT APARTMENTS... NEAR ESTES DRIVE IN CHAPEL HILL.
A PORTION OF HWY 70 NEAR COMMUNITY DR CLOSED DUE TO HIGH WATER. ALSO...HIGH WATER HAS CLOSED PART OF SPENCE AVE IN GOLDSBORO.
U.S. HIGHWAY 74 CLOSED FOR ABOUT 45 MINUTES. ONE LANE EACH WAY NOW OPEN.
MULTIPLE ROAD CLOSURES NEAR ROCKINGHAM AND WATER IN HOMES NEAR SPRINGDALE DRIVE AND NEAR MILL ROAD.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED IRREGULAR SHAPED HAIL UP TO BASEBALL SIZE.
GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL REPORTED IN CENTRAL NEWTON GROVE.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED 2IN SIZE HAIL IN NEWTON GROVE.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL AT THE INTERSECTION OF EDWARDS STORE ROAD AND RAYNORE MILL ROAD.
A REPORT OF HALF DOLLAR SIZE HAIL 3 MILES SOUTH OF GRANTHAM.
FIRE RESCUE REPORTED GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL NEAR HWY 117A.
A REPORT OF QUARTER SIZE HAIL AT US HWY 117 NEAR DUDLEY

THE MOST SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE WAS CONCENTRATED AT A DWELLING ALONG MAXWELL ROAD IN WESTERN SAMPSON COUNTY...WHERE TREES WERE SNAPPED...A CARPORT WAS BLOWN AND TWISTED INTO TREES...AND SHRUBS WERE PULLED FROM THE GROUND. NO INJURIES WERE REPORTED. THE ESTIMATED WIDTH OF THE TORNADO WAS 50 YARDS.
DAMAGE TO RESIDENCE AND BARN AT 4300 MAXWELL ROAD. DEBRIS IN ROAD. TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN AS WELL.
WIND DAMAGE AND DEBRIS REPORTED AT 12000 HARNETT/DUNN HIGHWAY.

THE TORNADO INITIALLY TOUCHED DOWN JUST OFF NOAH ROAD, WHERE THERE WERE SEVERAL UPROOTED TREES. IT CROSSED WOOD CROSSROADS, PRODUCING SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE TO ONE DOUBLE-WIDE MOBILE HOME AND TEARING THE PORCH OFF A SUBSTANTIAL BRICK SINGLE LEVEL HOME. THE TORNADO THEN CONTINUED NORTH-NORTHWEST PRODUCING MINOR DAMAGE ON FIVE POINTS ROAD TO A DOUBLE WIDE MOBILE HOME AND OTHER MINOR DAMAGE TO SHEDS AND STORAGE BUILDINGS. THE TOTAL PATH LENGTH WAS ONE MILE.
DAMAGE TO HOUSE AT WOODS CROSSROADS AND FIVE POINTS ROADS. BARN ALSO DESTROYED AND TREES DOWN. TIME EST BY LAW ENFORCEMENT AND RADAR.
COUNTY OFFICIALS REPORT DAMAGE IN SEVERAL LOCATIONS ACROSS JOHNSTON COUNTY. UNKNOWN NUMBER OF INJURIES REPORTED. RESCUE OPERATIONS IN PROGRESS.

THE TORNADO TRACKED FOR THREE MILES, INITIALLY TOUCHING DOWN NEAR DAUGHTRY ROAD SOUTH OF US HWY 70 AND FINALLY LIFTING ALONG COUNTRY STORE ROAD. A TRAVEL TRAILER WAS OVERTURNED AND SEVERAL HOMES SUFFERED SIGNIFICANT LOSS OF SHINGLES AND SIDING. THREE HOMES ALSO HAD THEIR CHIMNEYS DESTROYED BY THE WIND. OTHER DAMAGE TO A BARN AND HORSE TRAILER WAS NOTED ALONG WITH NUMEROUS DOWNED TREES.
LAW ENFORCEMENT OFFICIALS REPORT DAMAGE IN KENLY FROM THE TORNADO.

*** 1 FATAL, 2 INJ *** AT LEAST 10 HOMES WERE SEVERELY DAMAGED TO DESTROYED IN THE KENLY AREA NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF SCOTT ROAD AND BRIDGERS ROAD. CARS HAVE BEEN OVERTURNED. AS THE TORNADO CROSSED SCOTT ROAD, A DOUBLE-WIDE MOBILE HOME WAS REMOVED FROM ITS FOUNDATION AND FLIPPED APPROXIMATELY 50 FEET. A 60 YEAR OLD FEMALE OCCUPANT OF THIS MOBILE HOME WAS KILLED. TWO OTHER INJURIES WERE REPORTED. THE TORNADO PATH LENGTH WAS AROUND FOUR AND A HALF MILES.
HOMES DAMAGED IN LUCAMA.
EMERGENCY SERVICES ARE REPORTING ONE HOUSE DESTROYED ON FABWHITLEY ROAD. AT LEAST ONE MOBILE HAS BEEN OVERTURNED AND OTHERS HAVE SUSTAINED SEVERE DAMAGE IN THE AREA AROUND LUCAMA.

*** 1 FATAL, 4 INJ *** A HOUSE WAS DESTROYED AT 3850 LONDON CHURCH ROAD WHERE ONE FATALITLY WAS CONFIRMED. ACROSS THE ROAD FROM THIS HOME TO THE NORTHEAST...THE UPPER PORTION OF A HOME WAS DESTROYED...AND ANOTHER HOME HAD A ROOF COMPLETELY BLOWN AWAY WITH TREES ON TOP OF IT. OTHER HOMES AND BUILDINGS ALONG THE TORNADOS EIGHT MILE PATH SUFFERED MINOR TO MODERATE DAMAGE. NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER LINES WERE DOWNED AS WELL. FOUR INJURIES WERE REPORTED.
LAW ENFRCEMENT REPORT DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES AND SEARCH IS IN PROGRESS FOR RESIDENTS.
A LARGE TREE WAS UPROOTED AND SMASHED A PARKED VEHICLE ON DRAUGHN ROAD.
